Missing fact: Country: Qatar (The main country 'Qatar' is not stated except in program title, not clearly as country)
Missing fact: Agency: Al-Yousuf Enterprises LLP
Visual QA (catastrophic): No clear verification/trust footer with agency name/logo, RA/MEA number, presented with visual confidence; only small license detail as a line of microtext.
Visual QA (catastrophic): Contact CTA lacks prominence and is hard to locate at a glance.

Visual QA (catastrophic): No distinct, dominant candidate-facing hook: the headline is generic, and the UAE/Qatar/project/opportunity combination could be much more direct and bolder for instant recognition.
Visual QA (catastrophic): No visible agency identity—no logo, no verification band.
Visual QA score 73/100 is below the 85 publishable bar

Visual QA: Agency/verification identity elements (license details) are present but minimized and not clearly separated as a single trust footer—there is no logo, RA number, or MEA mark placed with trust emphasis.

Visual QA: The headline is prominent, but the candidate-facing hook ('QATAR CONSTRUCTION Earning opportunity') is split between lines and not as focused as best practice would dictate; the opportunity/country text could be more dominant.
Visual QA: Visual hierarchy could be stronger: job roles and CTA details collide slightly and do not maximize eye flow. The contact CTA is not visually prominent and blends with the job listing.
Visual QA: Main canvas is split into heavy top/bottom bands with large amounts of dense text—not as visually dynamic/engaging as competitive Gulf agency ads.
Visual QA: QR code feels like a sticker at the bottom instead of being organically integrated.

The headline is prominent, but the candidate-facing hook ('QATAR CONSTRUCTION Earning opportunity') is split between lines and not as focused as best practice would dictate; the opportunity/country text could be more dominant.

QR code feels like a sticker at the bottom instead of being organically integrated.
The benefits, walk-in, and contact information are all in a similarly sized small font—important contact CTA under-emphasized.
Too much similar text treatment: needs clearer typographic step between headline, subheadline, jobs, and critical CTA.

requiredCorrections.note: Increase the size, visual prominence, and separation of the contact CTA (phone/email) so it is immediately findable and visually dominant.
Add a proper trust/verification footer with agency logo, name, RA number, and MEA mark, not just as microtext, to meet industry trust conventions.
Increase the dominance and candidate-focus of the primary headline—recast as a unified, compelling hook (e.g. 'QATAR CONSTRUCTION — 500+ VACANCIES').
Integrate the QR code so it looks built into the footer/trust section, not floating on the composition.
